name: Radio Broadcast and Receiver
tagline: Modulated electromagnetic waves carrying voice and music — spectrum, antennas, and detectors at civilisation scale
problem:
Copper follows ships until it cannot; armies and orchestras want audiences beyond earshot. Radio encodes information on high-frequency carriers — amplitude or frequency modulation early, digital modes later — so one transmitter can serve many receivers without stringing private wires to each listener.

Physics intrudes: antennas want sizes comparable to wavelength; the ether is crowded; harmonics from sloppy transmitters jam distress frequencies. Receivers must select one story among megahertz of noise without deafening the operator.

Regulation becomes technology: licences, exams, band plans, and enforcement against spark-gap villains are as important as oscillator stability. Broadcasting reshaped politics and music — simultaneous audiences are a new social geometry.

Emergency services and marine distress remind us radio remains collapse-relevant even when phones feel universal — towers fall; skywave sometimes still answers.
overview:
Transmitters generate a stable carrier, modulate it with audio or symbols, amplify through linearised stages, and match impedance to an antenna so power leaves as radiation instead of reflected heat. Receivers heterodyne signals to a fixed intermediate frequency for easier filtering, apply automatic gain control for fading, and demodulate to baseband.

FM trades bandwidth for noise immunity; single-sideband squeezes spectrum for voice on crowded HF. Digital voice and data add error correction — intelligible speech where analogue would dissolve into hiss.

Antenna farms pattern energy toward cities; ionospheric HF reaches continents at night with moods set by solar weather. Studio-to-transmitter links moved from phone pairs to microwave STL; RDS embeds metadata on FM subcarriers.

Every link budget is a story of watts, antenna gain, path loss, and acceptable noise — engineering as accounting.

principles.name: Antenna reciprocity
principles.explanation: Transmit and receive patterns match for passive antennas — design once, use both ways.

principles.name: Superheterodyne image rejection
principles.explanation: Mixing creates sum and difference frequencies — front-end filtering must kill the image band.

principles.name: Capture effect in FM
principles.explanation: Stronger station captures demodulator — blessing and curse in overlapping markets.

principles.name: Shannon-Hartley limit
principles.explanation: Channel capacity grows with bandwidth and SNR — guides digital rollouts.

components.name: Power amplifier
components.madeFrom: Tube or solid-state pallets with combiners

rawMaterials.name: Aluminium tower sections
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Copper and aluminium for antennas and radials; PTFE coax where low loss matters — keep connectors weatherproofed

rawMaterials.name: Coaxial cable
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: High-permeability cores for IF transformers; air-core where Q must be high and stable

rawMaterials.name: Ferrite cores
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Quartz crystals or ceramic resonators for stable oscillators; rubidium references where budgets allow

buildSteps.title: Learn your national band plan before soldering
buildSteps.description: Print the allowed frequencies, power limits, and emission types for your licence class. Mark harmonics of your oscillator that must be suppressed — neighbours will not forgive interference to aeronautical bands.
buildSteps.warningNote: Transmitting illegally can cost lives as well as fines

buildSteps.title: Build or buy an antenna matched to your chosen wavelength
buildSteps.description: Measure SWR with a meter or bridge; trim elements patiently. Install lightning arrestors and proper earth bonds before first TX tests.
buildSteps.warningNote: RF burns are insidious — assume feedline is live

buildSteps.title: Assemble a superhet receiver with stable local oscillator
buildSteps.description: Shield oscillator stages; verify image rejection with a signal generator if available. Set AGC time constants so fading does not pump audio obnoxiously.
buildSteps.warningNote: Microphonics in coils sound like percussion

buildSteps.title: Align IF filters and demodulator for intended mode
buildSteps.description: For AM, check diode detector bias; for FM, verify discriminator symmetry; for SSB, set carrier insertion carefully. Log alignment screws touched — future alignment starts from known state.
buildSteps.warningNote: Wide IF sounds exciting until adjacent channels arrive

buildSteps.title: On-air tests with a mentor
buildSteps.description: Make short legal test transmissions; request signal reports; iterate antenna height and grounding. Keep a station log — it disciplines troubleshooting and proves good citizenship.
buildSteps.warningNote: Never test alone inside shielded rooms without external verification

history.event: Marconi experiments with wireless telegraphy; transatlantic spark follows.
Scheduled audio broadcasting begins in multiple countries.

inventors.name: Guglielmo Marconi
inventors.contribution: Practical wireless telegraphy and early radio systems

impact: Radio created national simultaneity — coronations, wars, and jazz in every kitchen. It also taught spectrum as finite commons — a tragedy-of-the-commons problem solved imperfectly by law.

videos.title: Superheterodyne receiver
videos.description: Ham radio licence training on mixing and IF.

sources.title: NASA — Communications and navigation (entry via nasa.gov)
sources.note: Deep-space communications engineering; navigate to mission directorate pages for technical reports
